 {"id":"bd-36vb","title":"§9.6-9.7 Trait Composition (Layer Connection) + Mock Implementations","description":"Covers §9.6 Trait Composition (How Layers Connect) and §9.7 Mock Implementations for Testing (spec lines 13083-13116). §9.6 specifies ownership and call-chain relationships between trait layers: (1) Vfs+VfsFile->Pager: Pager owns Box<dyn VfsFile> for the database file, opened via Vfs::open() during connection setup; (2) Pager+Wal->MvccPager: MvccPager wraps both, get_page() resolution chain is write_set->version_chain (MVCC version store)->disk (Pager checks WAL via WalIndex then reads database file); (3) MvccPager->BtCursor: cursor calls pager.get_page() during B-tree traversal, all page access goes through MVCC version resolution transparently; (4) BtCursor->VdbeCursor->VDBE: OpenRead opcode creates VdbeCursors wrapping BtCursors, Column opcode extracts fields via cursor chain; (5) VDBE+FunctionRegistry->Execution: Function/PureFunc opcodes look up functions in registry and call invoke()/step()/finalize(). §9.7 designates mock implementations for each trait: MockVfs/MockVfsFile (records all calls, returns configurable responses, used in pager tests to simulate I/O errors), MockMvccPager (returns pre-configured page data for given pgno+txn_id, used in B-tree tests to isolate from MVCC), MockBtreeCursor (returns pre-configured rows, used in VDBE tests), MockScalarFunction (returns fixed value, used in codegen tests). Sealed trait mock placement rule: for sealed traits (MvccPager, BtreeCursorOps), mocks MUST live in the defining crate (same crate as the private sealed supertrait) and are exported as types/values for other crates to use in tests. Unit tests required: test_pager_owns_vfs_file (accepts Box<dyn VfsFile>, uses for I/O), test_pager_opens_via_vfs (Vfs::open() called, VfsFile stored in Pager), test_mvcc_pager_page_resolution_chain (checks write_set first, then version_chain, then disk — MockVfs verifies disk only hit on miss), test_mvcc_pager_wraps_pager_and_wal (construction requires both, WAL frames checked before db file), test_btcursor_calls_pager_get_page (traversal ops call pager.get_page per page, tracked via MockMvccPager), test_vdbe_cursor_wraps_btcursor (OpenRead creates VdbeCursor wrapping BtCursor, Column extracts through chain), test_vdbe_function_lookup (Function/PureFunc opcodes look up and call invoke), test_mock_vfs_records_calls (open/delete/access recorded with correct params), test_mock_vfs_configurable_errors (I/O errors on specific ops, error propagation through Pager), test_mock_mvcc_pager_preconfigured_pages (correct data for pgno+txn_id pairs), test_mock_btree_cursor_preconfigured_rows (first/next/payload/rowid return expected values), test_mock_scalar_function_fixed_value (returns fixed SqliteValue regardless of input), test_sealed_trait_mock_in_defining_crate (MockMvccPager/MockBtreeCursor defined in sealed trait crate and exported), test_layer_isolation_btree_without_real_pager (MockMvccPager isolates B-tree logic), test_layer_isolation_vdbe_without_real_btree (MockBtreeCursor isolates VDBE logic). E2E: test_e2e_full_layer_stack (MemoryVfs->Pager->MvccPager->BtCursor->VdbeCursor->VDBE, INSERT then SELECT verifies data flows through all layers), test_e2e_mock_vfs_error_propagation (MockVfs fails on 3rd write, error propagates cleanly VfsFile->Pager->MvccPager->VDBE), test_e2e_function_registry_in_vdbe (register custom scalar, compile+execute SELECT calling it, verify invoked via Function opcode). Logging: DEBUG for layer-connection tracing (from_layer, to_layer, operation), INFO for integration test summary. Acceptance criteria: all five layer-connection relationships verified; all four mock types functional with configurable behavior; sealed trait mock placement rule enforced; layer isolation tests demonstrate testing each layer independently; full-stack E2E test passes from SQL text to result rows.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:03:21.204263847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T16:05:23.239779209Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T16:05:23.239749113Z","close_reason":"Completed: trait composition + mock implementation tests added and passing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-36vb","depends_on_id":"bd-1dc9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-36vb","depends_on_id":"bd-8kd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1205,"issue_id":"bd-36vb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Trait Composition (Layer Connection) + Mock Implementations

### Spec Content (Lines 13083-13116, sections 9.6-9.7)

**9.6 Trait Composition: How Layers Connect (lines 13083-13099)**

Specifies the ownership and call-chain relationships between trait layers:

1. **Vfs + VfsFile -> Pager**: The Pager owns a `Box<dyn VfsFile>` for the database file. Opens the file via `Vfs::open()` during connection setup.

2. **Pager + Wal -> MvccPager**: The MvccPager wraps both. `get_page()` resolution chain: write_set -> version_chain (MVCC version store) -> disk (Pager, which checks WAL via WalIndex, then reads from database file).

3. **MvccPager -> BtCursor**: Cursor calls `pager.get_page()` during B-tree traversal. All page access goes through MVCC version resolution transparently.

4. **BtCursor -> VdbeCursor -> VDBE**: VDBE opcodes like `OpenRead` create VdbeCursors wrapping BtCursors. `Column` opcode extracts fields via cursor.

5. **VDBE + FunctionRegistry -> Execution**: `Function`/`PureFunc` opcodes look up functions in the registry, call `invoke()`/`step()`/`finalize()`.

**9.7 Mock Implementations for Testing (lines 13101-13114)**

Each trait has a designated mock implementation:

- **MockVfs / MockVfsFile**: Records all calls, returns configurable responses. Used in pager tests to simulate I/O errors.
- **MockMvccPager**: Returns pre-configured page data for given `(pgno, txn_id)`. Used in B-tree tests to isolate from MVCC.
- **MockBtreeCursor**: Returns pre-configured rows. Used in VDBE tests.
- **MockScalarFunction**: Returns a fixed value. Used in codegen tests.

**Sealed trait mock placement rule** (line 13112): For sealed internal traits (e.g., `MvccPager`, `BtreeCursorOps`), mocks MUST live in the defining crate (the one that defines the private `sealed` supertrait). Other crates use the exported mock types/values rather than implementing the trait themselves.

### Unit Tests Required

1. **test_pager_owns_vfs_file**: Verify that the Pager accepts a `Box<dyn VfsFile>` and uses it for I/O operations (read, write, sync).
2. **test_pager_opens_via_vfs**: Verify that connection setup calls `Vfs::open()` and stores the returned `VfsFile` in the Pager.
3. **test_mvcc_pager_page_resolution_chain**: Verify `get_page()` checks write_set first, then version_chain, then falls through to disk Pager. Use MockVfs to verify disk is only hit when page is not in write_set or version_chain.
4. **test_mvcc_pager_wraps_pager_and_wal**: Verify MvccPager construction requires both a Pager and WAL component, and that WAL frames are checked before database file.
5. **test_btcursor_calls_pager_get_page**: Verify that B-tree cursor traversal operations (first, next, index_move_to) call `pager.get_page()` for each page they visit. Use MockMvccPager to track calls.
6. **test_vdbe_cursor_wraps_btcursor**: Verify `OpenRead` opcode creates a VdbeCursor that wraps a BtCursor, and `Column` opcode extracts data through the cursor chain.
7. **test_vdbe_function_lookup**: Verify `Function`/`PureFunc` opcodes look up functions in the FunctionRegistry and call `invoke()`.
8. **test_mock_vfs_records_calls**: Create a MockVfs, call `open`, `delete`, `access`, verify all calls are recorded with correct parameters.
9. **test_mock_vfs_configurable_errors**: Configure MockVfs to return I/O errors on specific operations, verify error propagation through the Pager.
10. **test_mock_mvcc_pager_preconfigured_pages**: Set up MockMvccPager with page data for specific `(pgno, txn_id)` pairs, verify correct data is returned by `get_page()`.
11. **test_mock_btree_cursor_preconfigured_rows**: Set up MockBtreeCursor with rows, verify `first/next/payload/rowid` return expected values.
12. **test_mock_scalar_function_fixed_value**: Create MockScalarFunction returning a fixed SqliteValue, verify `invoke` returns it regardless of input.
13. **test_sealed_trait_mock_in_defining_crate**: Verify that mock implementations for sealed traits (MockMvccPager, MockBtreeCursor) are defined in the same crate as the sealed trait and are exported for use by other crates' tests.
14. **test_layer_isolation_btree_without_real_pager**: Use MockMvccPager to test B-tree logic (splits, merges, seeks) without any real I/O or MVCC complexity.
15. **test_layer_isolation_vdbe_without_real_btree**: Use MockBtreeCursor to test VDBE execution logic without real B-tree traversal.

### E2E Tests

**test_e2e_full_layer_stack**: Construct the full layer stack: MemoryVfs -> Pager -> MvccPager -> BtCursor -> VdbeCursor -> VDBE. Execute a simple `INSERT` followed by `SELECT` and verify the data flows correctly through all layers from SQL text to result rows.

**test_e2e_mock_vfs_error_propagation**: Use MockVfs configured to fail on the 3rd write. Execute multiple INSERTs and verify the error propagates cleanly from VfsFile::write through Pager through MvccPager to the VDBE, which returns the appropriate error to the caller.

**test_e2e_function_registry_in_vdbe**: Register a custom scalar function in the FunctionRegistry, compile and execute a SELECT that calls it, verify the function is invoked via the VDBE's Function opcode and the result appears in the query output.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:30:19Z"},{"id":1206,"issue_id":"bd-36vb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Logging Requirements

- DEBUG: layer-connection tracing: `from_layer`, `to_layer`, `operation`.
- INFO: integration test summary for trait composition.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T07:41:19Z"},{"id":1207,"issue_id":"bd-36vb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

- [ ] Sealed traits (MvccPager, BtreeCursorOps) cannot be implemented downstream (private module pattern)
- [ ] User-implementable traits (Vfs, VfsFile, ScalarFunction, etc.) can be implemented by external crates
- [ ] Layer connection: Pager owns Box<dyn VfsFile>, MvccPager wraps Pager, BtCursor uses MvccPager
- [ ] Mock implementations provided for all sealed traits (MockMvccPager, MockBtreeCursor) in defining crate
- [ ] Mock implementations exported for downstream test use
- [ ] Recording/verifiable call sequences: mock tracks method calls for assertion
- [ ] I/O error injection: MockVfsFile can simulate read/write failures
- [ ] All trait method errors propagate as Result<T, FrankenError> with correct error codes
","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:52:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3704r","title":"Session 26: SQL Codegen Correctness Fixes and Probe Tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T15:26:12.357704608Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T15:26:37.768104733Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T15:26:37.768081700Z","close_reason":"Complete: 4 codegen fixes, 30 new tests, 1286+358 tests passing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1208,"issue_id":"bd-3704r","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Session 26: SQL Codegen Correctness Fixes and Probe Tests

### Changes

#### 1. JsonAccess -> json_extract rewrite (new feature)
- `expr -> '$.path'` and `expr ->> '$.path'` now emit `PureFunc json_extract`
  instead of silently returning NULL via the catch-all arm
- 3 new tests: double arrow extract, arrow number, arrow in WHERE clause

#### 2. WHERE col = NULL NULLEQ bug (correctness fix, bd-1t1dk)
- Added `IsNull val_reg, skip_label` guard before `Ne` with NULLEQ (0x80) in
  `emit_where_filter` Eq fast-path
- Root cause: NULLEQ makes NULL==NULL true (IS semantics), but SQL `=` treats
  NULL=NULL as UNKNOWN (false in WHERE)
- Affects both column-on-left and column-on-right branches
- 1 regression test

#### 3. COLLATE propagation in comparisons (correctness fix, bd-28ev5)
- `emit_comparison()`: added `extract_collation()` helper, passes P4::Collation
  to comparison opcodes when either operand has a COLLATE wrapper
- `resolve_column_ref()`: unwraps Expr::Collate to reach inner Column, so
  COLLATE expressions can use the Eq fast-path
- `emit_where_filter` Eq fast-path: extracts and propagates collation to Ne
- Fixes pre-existing failure: collate_in_join_does_not_return_null

#### 4. OR fast-path in emit_where_filter (optimization)
- Added dedicated OR handler: left_skip label + pass_label pattern
- Enables Eq fast-path for each side of OR independently
- 2 existing tests exercise this path (test_where_or_condition,
  test_where_or_with_null)

#### 5. 30 new probe/regression tests
- Aggregates on empty table (count=0, sum/max/min=NULL)
- NULL comparison semantics (=, >, <, !=, >=, <=)
- INSERT SELECT with expressions
- DELETE with complex WHERE
- Nested function calls (upper(substr()))
- count(col) vs count(*)
- GROUP BY with HAVING (sum filter)
- Multiple aggregates in GROUP BY
- != and <> operators
- OR conditions with NULL
- SELECT DISTINCT
- COALESCE with all NULL
- LIMIT/OFFSET boundary conditions
- Unary minus
- String concatenation (||)
- NOT IN, NOT LIKE, NOT BETWEEN
- JOIN patterns: inner, left, self, cross, with WHERE

### Files Changed
- `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/codegen.rs` — JsonAccess emit, IsNull guard, COLLATE
  extraction, resolve_column_ref unwrap, OR fast-path, Eq collation propagation
- `crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs` — 30 new tests

### Quality Gates
- 1286 fsqlite-core tests pass (was 1256, +30 new)
- 358 fsqlite tests pass (was 357+1 failure, fixed collate bug)
- cargo clippy (pedantic+nursery) clean
- cargo fmt clean

### Beads Closed
- bd-1t1dk: WHERE col = NULL NULLEQ flag bug
- bd-28ev5: COLLATE in comparison expressions loses collation info

 {"id":"bd-37kc","title":"Corruption & Recovery: deterministic corruption + SQLite loss + FrankenSQLite repair","description":"## Goal

Create dramatic, reproducible corruption demos that:

- corrupt working copies of real DB fixtures deterministically
- demonstrate what stock SQLite does (cannot open, data loss, or partial salvage)
- demonstrate FrankenSQLite recovery using WAL-FEC / RaptorQ repair symbols

## Required Properties

- Deterministic: scenario + seed fully determine the corrupted bytes.
- Safe: never corrupt originals or golden fixtures.
- Evidence-rich: always produce a proof bundle:
  - sha256 before/after
  - corruption description (what, where)
  - seed
  - which pages/frames were affected
  - sqlite error codes and integrity_check output
  - FrankenSQLite recovery logs + decode proof artifacts

## Outputs

- A scenario catalog suitable for:
  - CI-smoke (small subset)
  - local demos (full set)
- A narrative demo runner (human-readable walkthrough).
- A structured JSON output mode for programmatic consumption.

## Acceptance

- For at least one real fixture + corruption pattern, baseline SQLite demonstrably loses data / fails to open, while FrankenSQLite recovers to Tier2/Tier1 equivalence.
- Demos are easy to run and produce stable output directories.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:24:01.009034948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:47:20.893292692Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:47:20.893277663Z","close_reason":"Duplicate plan: use canonical RealDB epic bd-2als (with detailed sub-epics/tasks + acceptance criteria).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-37kj","title":"Implement CREATE INDEX, CREATE VIEW, and DROP INDEX/VIEW support","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-11T08:16:59.075604467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T08:28:42.894301493Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T08:28:42.894276626Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-37yfc","title":"View SELECT * column resolution fails (same as CTE SELECT * bug)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-27T23:13:26.867379788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T23:13:36.874073228Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T23:13:36.874040086Z","close_reason":"Fixed: applied resolve_cte_column_names_with_schema to view and CREATE TABLE AS SELECT paths","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1209,"issue_id":"bd-37yfc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## View SELECT * Column Resolution Bug — Fixed

Same root cause as the CTE bug (bd-1ukm6). Views created with `SELECT * FROM table` had their columns stored as literal `"*"` instead of actual column names. Applied `resolve_cte_column_names_with_schema()` to view materialization path (connection.rs:5359) and CREATE TABLE AS SELECT path (connection.rs:4453).

### Test
conformance_028_create_select_drop_view passes: creates a view with SELECT *, then queries by column name.
","created_at":"2026-02-27T23:13:36Z"}]}
